title: Barbados: Strengthening disaster resilience of SMEs through business continuity planning: Guidance and templates
content: This document provides guidance for Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (MSMEs) in Barbados, including micro enterprises, in developing a basic Business Continuity Plan, eventually aligning with the highest international standards.

title: Barbados Chamber of Commerce and Industry (BCCI)
content: The Barbados Chamber of Commerce and Industry (BCCI) provides its members with focused leadership and a strong voice to promote the interests of Barbados’ business sector.
snippet: The Barbados Chamber of Commerce and Industry (BCCI) provides its members with focused leadership and a strong voice to promote the interests of Barbados’ business sector.

title: Survivors sue Indonesian government over response to catastrophic Sumatra floods
content: A group of Indonesian citizens affected by the late-2025 Sumatra floods and landslides have filed a lawsuit with a court in Jakarta in an effort to hold the Indonesian government accountable for what they describe as an “ecological disaster.”

title: The case for vehicle-integrated photovoltaics in disaster zones
content: A new IEA PVPS report makes a rigorous case that solar electric vehicles are not just transport, but rather a distributed, mobile, fuel-free emergency energy network that could redefine disaster preparedness.

title: What desert locusts reveal about disaster monitoring
content: Early detection systems can dramatically reduce long-term harms to humans, study finds.
snippet: Early detection systems can dramatically reduce long-term harms to humans, study finds.

title: From reaction to prevention: using remittances to act before climate crises hit
content: An initiative by the H&M Foundation and Mercy Corps and co-funded by global financial technology platform, Adyen, is testing how early-warning systems can trigger remittances before climate disasters strike.

title: H&M Foundation
content: H&M Foundation supports the industry in halving its greenhouse gas emissions every decade by 2050, while promoting a just transition for both people and the planet.
snippet: supports the industry in halving its greenhouse gas emissions every decade by 2050, while promoting a just transition for both people and the planet.

title: Building finds its balance in groundbreaking earthquake test
content: A timber building that kept itself centred through major shaking has aced a full‑scale earthquake test with no damage, with more than 60 industry professionals looking on.
snippet: A timber building that kept itself centred through major shaking has aced a full‑scale earthquake test with no damage, with more than 60 industry professionals looking on.

title: The next chapter in flood resilience: Open sourcing Google’s hydrology framework
content: Google has open-sourced its hydrology model to enable National Meteorological and Hydrological Services to integrate advanced AI-based flood forecasting into their own workflows.
snippet: Google has open-sourced its hydrology model to enable National Meteorological and Hydrological Services to integrate advanced AI-based flood forecasting into their own workflows.

title: Heat is a growing threat to the Hajj—even in spring
content: Temperatures during the pilgrimage in Saudi Arabia are rising as climate change accelerates, according to a growing body of research.
snippet: Temperatures during the pilgrimage in Saudi Arabia are rising as climate change accelerates, according to a growing body of research.

title: Conceptualizing urban heat resilience through capacities and a multidimensional perspective: Empirical evidence from formal and informal settlements of Lahore, Pakistan
content: This index-based study examines the heatwave resilience of both formal and informal communities located in Lahore, Pakistan. Resilience is assessed in terms of its capacities and dimensions.
snippet: This index-based study examines the heatwave resilience of both formal and informal communities located in Lahore, Pakistan. Resilience is assessed in terms of its capacities and dimensions.

title: Developing a rapid earthquake impact assessment procedure at European scale
content: This study introduces a comprehensive rapid earthquake impact assessment procedure at the European scale, designed to provide timely damage and loss estimates following seismic events.
snippet: This study introduces a comprehensive rapid earthquake impact assessment procedure at the European scale, designed to provide timely damage and loss estimates following seismic events.

title: Applying a climate information distillation framework to support a climate resilient hydropower sector in Nepal
content: This study applies the Climate Information Distillation Framework (CIDF) to the Nepal hydropower sector.
snippet: This study applies the Climate Information Distillation Framework (CIDF) to the Nepal hydropower sector.

title: Beyond hazards and sectors: governing systemic climate risks
content: The report uses an exploratory comparative analysis of adaptation governance across selected countries and US states to examine both the recognition of systemic risks.
snippet: The report uses an exploratory comparative analysis of adaptation governance across selected countries and US states to examine both the recognition of systemic risks.

title: Post-Disaster Needs Assessment (PDNA) Report: Cyclone Ditwah, Sri Lanka 2025
content: This is a comprehensive assessment that will guide Sri Lanka's recovery and reconstruction efforts following one of the most devastating disasters in its recent history.
snippet: This is a comprehensive assessment that will guide Sri Lanka's recovery and reconstruction efforts following one of the most devastating disasters in its recent history.

title: Heatwaves scorched their harvests. Now, these farmers are saving Nigeria’s Yam Belt
content: In 2024, communities in northern Nigeria saw temperatures hit a record of over 40°C. But in late 2025, to save their crops, farmers across several Zing communities turned to a practice which involves covering their yam ridges with dried grasses.

title: The Role of Insurance in Climate Change Adaptation: Current Practices and Future Challenges

title: Bank of Greece
content: The Bank of Greece is the national central bank for Greece within the Eurosystem. It was the Greek central bank from 1927 to 2000, issuing the drachma.
snippet: The Bank of Greece is the national central bank for Greece within the Eurosystem. It was the Greek central bank from 1927 to 2000, issuing the drachma.

title: Launch of COPE’s book: Lightning

snippet: Join for the online launch of COPE’s newest book, Lightning, created to help children and families understand one of nature’s most powerful hazards and learn how to stay safe during thunderstorms.

title: Assessing the real-world economic value of weather forecasts under compounding extremes: a decision-specific framework
content: This study proposes a flexible framework to assess the economic value of weather forecasts, with penalty functions that explicitly account for compounding losses as well as declining user trust in cases of repeated false alarms.

title: Understanding the experiences of persons with disabilities in disasters and emergencies in Tonga (December 2025)

title: Public warning and evacuation experiences during recent GLOF events (2019 and 2023) and recommendations for future preparedness: Insights from Lunana, Bhutan

title: Lao PDR ties drought insurance payouts to early warning triggers
content: When drought indicators in Lao PDR cross a defined level, money moves. That is the core logic behind a new anticipatory drought insurance pilot launched May 29, 2026.
snippet: When drought indicators in Lao PDR cross a defined level, money moves. That is the core logic behind a new anticipatory drought insurance pilot launched May 29, 2026.

title: Prominent theory of atmospheric waves may not explain extreme weather patterns
content: Extreme weather events have increased in frequency and severity. A study shows that one proposed partial explanation may not be capable of explaining any of this increase in severe weather events.
snippet: Extreme weather events have increased in frequency and severity. A study shows that one proposed partial explanation may not be capable of explaining any of this increase in severe weather events.

title: Intersec Deploys Kuwait’s public warning system in less than 10 days

title: Disaster Risk Reduction Summer School 2026

snippet: Join leading DRR experts for a five‑day summer school exploring why disasters are not natural, how human decisions drive risk, and what effective, accountable disaster reduction looks like.

title: Can green infrastructure also protect foundations?
content: Dutch cities are increasingly facing foundation problems as a result of low groundwater levels, shrinking clay soils and land subsidence. Climate change is exacerbating these effects.
snippet: Dutch cities are increasingly facing foundation problems as a result of low groundwater levels, shrinking clay soils and land subsidence. Climate change is exacerbating these effects.
